title: senecio amphibolus var. socialis
intro: Senecio amphibolus var. socialis, also known as Groundsel, is a flowering plant species within the daisy family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its ability to induce sweating and relieve coughs, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for respiratory conditions such as bronchitis and asthma. Groundsel can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
